mission.title: sops: README.rst is not correctly rendered
mission.description: README.rst is not correctly rendered. Requested by 40+ users.

steps.title: Check current sops setup
steps.description:
Verify your sops version and configuration:
```bash
sops version
```
This feature requires a working sops installation.

steps.title: Review sops configuration
steps.description: Review the relevant sops configuration: Screenshot:

steps.title: Apply the fix for README.rst is not correctly rendered
steps.description:
There are multiple PRs open that modify README.rst, we shouldn't start splitting it up / moving it around / converting it to MD / ... before these are processed.

So here's a potential interim solution: convert the RST to MarkDown automatically using pandoc.

Unfortunately pandoc doesn't handle everything, including the table of contents and notes, which we both use :-(

I'm currently working on some RST to MD conversion code (using Python docutils) anyway for Ansible changelogs, once I find

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade sops to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade sops:
```bash
# Check current version
sops version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/getsops/sops
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "README.rst is not correctly rendered" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
There are multiple PRs open that modify README.rst, we shouldn't start splitting it up / moving it around / converting it to MD / ... before these are processed.

So here's a potential interim solution: convert the RST to MarkDown automatically using pandoc.

prerequisites.description: A working sops installation or development environment.
